intro: Fernandezia putumayoensis is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Fernandezia putumayoensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in a decoction, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to create remedies for fever reduction, digestive issues, and wound healing.
